rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the paper excerpt's thesis, framing, and contribution claim.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what one thinks of the artifact centrally includes checking whether the motivation, method, baselines, results, and positioning cohere logically.
rationale.R1.3: The excerpt makes quantitative claims about ECE, WER, and reliability diagrams, so claim-evidence alignment is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact explicitly claims to extend prior calibration work and be the first systematic study in a specific area, making novelty assessment central.
rationale.R1.7: Named prior works and claims about standards, baselines, and recent lines of work make citation and scholarship assessment central.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague evaluative delegation must preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and standards of review.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims about standards, strongest baselines, and firstness, so overclaim avoidance is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within responsibility projection and avoid actually reviewing or improving the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Because the excerpt concerns ASR confidence in downstream agent routing, harmful implications of overconfident or unsupported claims remain relevant.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's explicit claims.
